library_meta.name: IMS Base Position Library
library_meta.methodology: FRS / Kinstretch / IMS hybrid - base positions as programming containers
library_meta.philosophy: Base positions are the primary unit of FRC programming at IMS. A session's 'Base Position Block' is structured around 1-2 positions with all the modalities pulled from underlying libraries: CARs, PAILs/RAILs, end-range isos, hovers, ramping, and position-specific coaching work. The position is the container; the modalities are what lives inside it.

builder_integration.session_block: Base Position Block (0-20 min depending on session length)
builder_integration.selection_rule: Builder selects 1-2 positions per session based on assessment findings + block intent
builder_integration.block_content_source: Position file contains references to CARs, PAILs/RAILs from existing libraries + live-in work native to the position

long_sit.name: Long Sit
long_sit.description: Seated on the floor with legs extended straight in front, feet flexed, spine tall and stacked over pelvis. Foundational seated position that tests passive hamstring length, hip flexion at extended knee, and seated spine control simultaneously. Most adults struggle to sit tall here without rounding - that's the diagnostic.
setup_cues: Legs extended straight, feet flexed (dorsiflexed)
Knees soft (not locked)
Sit bones rooted, pelvis neutral
Spine tall - imagine a string lifting the crown of the head
Hands rest on thighs or on the floor beside the hips
primary_capacities_trained: Hip flexion at extended knee (hamstring length)
long_sit.entry_criteria: Can sit on floor. Can extend legs without acute pain. Hamstring tolerance sufficient to hold the shape briefly.
contraindications: Acute low back pain (particularly disc-related flexion-intolerant)
Recent knee surgery preventing full extension

regressions.name: Elevated Long Sit (block under sit bones)
regressions.when_to_use: Pelvis tips back, spine rounds hard. Elevate to allow pelvis to stay neutral.

regressions.name: Long Sit with Bent Knees
regressions.when_to_use: Hamstring restriction too severe for full extension. Bend knees, feet flat - still trains seated spine control.

regressions.name: Long Sit Against Wall
regressions.when_to_use: Client needs tactile feedback for spine position. Back against wall.

progressions.name: Long Sit Forward Fold
progressions.what_it_adds: Loads the hamstring and spine flexion under gravity
progressions.gates_to_move_up: Owns upright long sit for 60s with neutral pelvis

progressions.name: Long Sit Active Leg Lifts
progressions.what_it_adds: Active hip flexion + quad engagement at end range. Tests what's active range vs passive.

progressions.name: Long Sit with Toes Pointed
progressions.what_it_adds: Changes the demand - now plantarflexion + hamstring length. Different neurological pattern.

cars_available.notes: Segmental spine work from long sit - legs fixed forces pelvis stability
T-spine rotation with extended legs

pails_rails_targets_available.how_it_sets_up_in_this_position: Long sit loads hamstring length at end hip flexion with knee extended. PAIL = press legs down into floor (hamstrings contract against stretched position). RAIL = lift legs off floor actively (hip flexors drive deeper flexion).
pails_rails_targets_available.level_programming: L1-L2 - hamstring work usually conservative intro

end_range_iso_positions.description: Active hamstring hold - legs pressed firmly into floor, 20-45s

hovers_available.description: Hover legs 1-2 inches off floor while maintaining pelvis neutral. Active hip flexion.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Slow Forward Fold
live_in_the_position_work.description: Hinge forward from hips, not spine. Hands walk toward shins/feet.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Active Leg Press-Down
live_in_the_position_work.description: Press legs actively into floor - quad and hamstring engage.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Ankle Pump
live_in_the_position_work.description: In long sit, flex and point the feet. Ankle mobility + hamstring variation.
live_in_the_position_work.purpose: Ankle DF/PF capacity + lower leg integration

live_in_the_position_work.name: Arm Reach Overhead (spine extension demand)
live_in_the_position_work.description: Reach arms overhead in long sit. Tests spine + hip flexion simultaneously.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Spine Rotation from Long Sit
live_in_the_position_work.description: Rotate torso to look behind. Legs stay fixed, spine does the work.

typical_block_dose.beginner_client: 5-8 min - introduce position, breath, gentle fold
typical_block_dose.intermediate_client: 8-12 min - CARs + PAIL/RAIL + fold + active work
typical_block_dose.advanced_client: 10-15 min - full integration with active leg work and rotations

flow_partners.why_pairs: Long sit tests hamstring (extended knee); butterfly tests hip ER (bent knee). Complementary.
Long sit legs together; straddle legs apart. Progression.
Long sit bilateral extended; 90/90 unilateral flexed+rotated. Different capacity dimensions.
Supine removes gravity on spine - good transition out of long sit if fatigued

prescribe_this_position_when: Hamstring length / hip flexion restriction flagged
Re-test position for hamstring ROM improvements
pair_with_external_strength_work: Hinge pattern blocks - long sit opens hamstrings for RDL work
Any lower body session with hamstring tightness flagged

butterfly.name: Butterfly
butterfly.description: Seated with soles of the feet together, knees falling out to the sides. Bilateral hip ER + abduction + flexion. Spine upright, hands on feet or ankles. The foundational adductor/hip opener - simple position, profound demand on hip external rotators and adductor lengthening.
setup_cues: Soles of feet pressed together, drawn toward the pelvis (not forced)
Let the knees fall naturally - don't force them toward the floor
Sit tall, ribs stacked over pelvis
Hands cupping the feet or resting on ankles - not pulling
Breathe - the shape releases on exhale, tightens on hold
butterfly.entry_criteria: Can sit on the floor with hips level for 30 seconds. If knees are up by the ears (very restricted), use elevation regressions before full butterfly.
contraindications: Acute knee pain (butterfly loads the medial knee)

regressions.name: Elevated Butterfly (block under sit bones)
regressions.when_to_use: Pelvis tips backward, spine rounds. Block/folded mat elevates the sit bones.

regressions.name: Supported Knees Butterfly
regressions.when_to_use: Knees float very high, adductor tightness prevents floor contact. Place blocks/pillows under knees.

regressions.name: Supine Butterfly
regressions.when_to_use: Client can't sit in the shape. Lie on back, soles together, knees out. Gravity-assisted.

regressions.name: Feet Further From Pelvis
regressions.when_to_use: Full butterfly (feet at pelvis) too intense. Move feet 12-18 inches away.

progressions.name: Butterfly Forward Fold
progressions.what_it_adds: Loads the capsule under gravity, spine flexion with hip demand
progressions.gates_to_move_up: Owns upright butterfly for 60s without knee pain

progressions.name: Butterfly with PAIL Press (knees into floor)
progressions.what_it_adds: Active adductor engagement while in abducted position - isometric ramping for adductor capacity
progressions.gates_to_move_up: Owns butterfly fold. Adductor tolerance cleared.

progressions.name: Butterfly with RAIL Drive (knees to floor)
progressions.what_it_adds: Active hip abduction at end range - drives adductor lengthening neurologically

cars_available.notes: Modified hip CARs from butterfly position - legs explore in abducted/ER position
cars_available.use_when: Client new to seated hip work

pails_rails_targets_available.how_it_sets_up_in_this_position: Butterfly loads adductor lengthening bilaterally. PAIL = press knees down into the floor (adductor contracts in stretched position). RAIL = actively pull knees toward floor (abductors drive deeper).
pails_rails_targets_available.level_programming: L1-L2 for most clients. Adductor-heavy work, respect tolerance.

pails_rails_targets_available.how_it_sets_up_in_this_position: Butterfly's ER component allows mild anterior capsule loading - less intense than 90/90 or pigeon, good teaching position.
pails_rails_targets_available.level_programming: L1 typically - butterfly is often the introduction to capsular work

end_range_iso_positions.description: Active press knees toward floor, hold 20-45s - adductor + hip abductor iso work
end_range_iso_positions.progression_code: PRH -> PRLO (adding overhead load or forward fold)

ramping_opportunities.description: Graded adductor effort 20-80% while in butterfly - indicated for adductor spasm finding

live_in_the_position_work.name: Forward Fold (slow descent)
live_in_the_position_work.description: Walk hands forward, spine rounds, fold into butterfly. Find the edge of the stretch, breathe.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Side-to-Side Weight Shift
live_in_the_position_work.description: Shift weight to one sit bone, then the other, feeling one hip load more than the other.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Spine Rotation in Butterfly
live_in_the_position_work.description: Keeping legs in butterfly, rotate the torso one direction then the other.
live_in_the_position_work.purpose: T-spine rotation from a locked hip base

live_in_the_position_work.name: Knee Press-Down (active adductor work)
live_in_the_position_work.description: Press knees actively toward the floor. Release. Repeat. Not a stretch - active engagement.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Breath Into the Adductors
live_in_the_position_work.description: Hands on inner thighs, breath expanded into the adductors. Felt awareness of the tissue.

typical_block_dose.beginner_client: 5-8 min - introduce position, forward fold, breath
typical_block_dose.advanced_client: 12-18 min - multi-modal with deep live-in and ramping

flow_partners.why_pairs: Butterfly loads adductors at short length; straddle loads them long. Complementary.
Butterfly is bilateral, 90/90 is asymmetric - sequence from general to specific
Long sit balances butterfly's hip ER focus with hip flexion at extended knees
Butterfly warms bilateral ER, pigeon takes it unilateral and deeper

prescribe_this_position_when: Hip ER restriction that needs a gentle teaching entry
Introduction to seated hip work for new clients
pair_with_external_strength_work: Squat pattern blocks - butterfly opens adductors for Cossack squats and wide-stance loading
Lateral movement days - butterfly prepares frontal plane work
General warm-up for lower body sessions

straddle.name: Straddle
straddle.description: Seated with legs extended wide to the sides, feet flexed, spine tall. Loads adductor lengthening with knees straight - different adductor demand than butterfly. Hip flexion with abduction + spine control. A challenging position for most adult clients due to adductor restriction.
setup_cues: Legs extended wide apart, toes pointing straight up (feet flexed)
Knees face up (don't let them rotate in or out)
Sit bones rooted, pelvis neutral
Spine tall - tendency is to round forward, resist that
Hands can rest on floor in front or on thighs
primary_capacities_trained: Hip abduction at end range (with knee extended)
Adductor length (straight-knee demand - different than butterfly)
Seated spine control in wide-leg position
straddle.entry_criteria: Can sit in long sit comfortably. Can abduct legs at least 60 degrees. Adductor tolerance at full knee extension.

regressions.name: Elevated Straddle (block under sit bones)
regressions.when_to_use: Pelvis tips back hard in wide-leg seated

regressions.name: Narrow Straddle
regressions.when_to_use: Adductor restriction prevents wide abduction. Start with legs at 45 degrees apart, widen over time.

regressions.name: Supine Straddle (legs on wall)
regressions.when_to_use: Seated is too intense. Lie on back, legs up wall, let them open into straddle gravity-assisted.

progressions.name: Straddle Forward Fold (center)
progressions.what_it_adds: Loads adductor length under gravity + spine flexion
progressions.gates_to_move_up: Owns upright straddle at moderate width for 60s

progressions.name: Straddle Side Bend (reach to one foot)
progressions.what_it_adds: Adds anti-lateral flexion + side-specific adductor loading

progressions.name: Straddle Press-Downs (active adduction)
progressions.what_it_adds: Active adductor engagement while in abducted position - the PAIL for adductors

cars_available.notes: Modified from straddle - each leg moves in abducted position
cars_available.use_when: Hip ROM exploration in abducted position

pails_rails_targets_available.how_it_sets_up_in_this_position: Straddle loads adductor lengthening at end abduction with extended knee - differs from butterfly (bent knee). PAIL = press legs down into floor (adductors contract in stretched position). RAIL = pull legs wider (abductors drive deeper abduction).
pails_rails_targets_available.level_programming: L1-L2 - straddle is intense adductor demand, respect tolerance

pails_rails_targets_available.how_it_sets_up_in_this_position: Straddle fold loads hip flexion at abducted + extended knee - unique capacity demand
pails_rails_targets_available.level_programming: L1-L2

end_range_iso_positions.description: Press legs actively into floor while in straddle - isometric adductor + hip flexion hold

hovers_available.description: Hover legs 1-2 inches off floor from straddle - active hip abduction + flexion

ramping_opportunities.description: Graded adductor effort while in straddle - 20-80% ramps

live_in_the_position_work.name: Slow Forward Fold
live_in_the_position_work.description: From straddle, fold forward from the hips. Adductor + spine loading.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Side Reach (one hand to one foot)
live_in_the_position_work.description: Reach one hand toward the same-side foot. Anti-lateral flexion + unilateral adductor.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Active Adductor Press-Downs
live_in_the_position_work.description: Press legs firmly into floor. Adductors contract at length.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Spine Rotation (left and right)
live_in_the_position_work.description: Rotate torso one direction then the other in straddle.
live_in_the_position_work.purpose: Spine rotation with stable wide-leg base

live_in_the_position_work.name: Breath Work at Edge
live_in_the_position_work.description: In deepest accessible straddle, deep breaths. Parasympathetic drive.

typical_block_dose.advanced_client: 12-15 min - full work with active holds and ramping

flow_partners.why_pairs: Butterfly (bent knee) and straddle (extended knee) load adductors differently - sequence them
Long sit tests hamstring in midline; straddle tests hamstring + adductor in abduction
Straddle prepares the passive adductor length; Cossack loads it actively
Straddle bilateral abduction; 90/90 unilateral ER. Different dimensions of hip capacity.

prescribe_this_position_when: Preparation for Cossack squats or wide-stance lower body work
pair_with_external_strength_work: Cossack squat / lateral squat progression days
Any lower body session with adductor tightness

90_90.description: Seated on the floor with both knees bent to 90 degrees - front leg rotated externally (shin horizontal across the body, thigh in front), back leg rotated internally (thigh out to the side, shin behind). Pelvis stays upright and square to the front shin. Tall spine, hands on the floor or knees for support.
setup_cues: Sit tall - imagine a string pulling the crown of the head up
Front shin parallel to the front of the mat, knee at 90, foot flexed
Back leg mirrors: knee at 90, thigh pointed straight out to the side
Both sit bones aim to stay grounded - lift whichever one wants to float
Hands can be on thighs, knees, or supporting behind you
90_90.entry_criteria: Can sit on the floor with hips reasonably level for 30 seconds without significant pain or pelvis tipping 45+ degrees. If client can't get into the shape at all, use regression options below.
contraindications: Acute knee pain or recent knee surgery (position loads both knees end-range)
Acute hip labral flare (position compresses the hip capsule)
Acute low back pain with radicular symptoms

regressions.name: Elevated 90/90 (on a block or folded mat)
regressions.when_to_use: Client can't sit upright in 90/90 without pelvis tipping hard backward. Yoga block or 2-3 inches of elevation under the sit bones.
regressions.rebuilds_to: Full 90/90 once hip ROM and pelvic control improve

regressions.name: Wall-Supported 90/90
regressions.when_to_use: Client tips backward without support. Back against the wall, legs in the shape.

regressions.name: Modified 90/90 (one leg at a time)
regressions.when_to_use: Client can only tolerate one hip in position at a time. Front leg in position, back leg straight behind. Switch sides to address the other hip.

regressions.name: Cross-Legged Seated (Easy Sit)
regressions.when_to_use: Client can't tolerate 90/90 shape at all. Start with a symmetric cross-legged position to train spine control and hip ER baseline.

progressions.name: 90/90 Lift-Off (front leg)
progressions.what_it_adds: Active hip ER capacity at end range - lifting the front shin off the floor while maintaining position
progressions.gates_to_move_up: Owns static 90/90 for 2+ minutes. No pelvic shift when lift-off attempted.

progressions.name: 90/90 Rocking (dynamic transitions)
progressions.what_it_adds: Active switching between sides - hip ER to IR demand under load
progressions.gates_to_move_up: Owns static 90/90 bilaterally. Can transition both directions without hands.

progressions.name: 90/90 with External Load (KB or plate)
progressions.what_it_adds: Axial loading of the seated position - forces deeper pelvic control
progressions.gates_to_move_up: Owns static 90/90 + lift-off. Used in capacity blocks only.

cars_available.notes: Movement-focused hip CAR from the 90/90 position. Each leg trains different articular demand.
cars_available.use_when: Client new to 90/90 or assessment shows aberrant hip CARs

cars_available.notes: Articular focus hip CAR - front leg works ER in flexion, back leg works IR in flexion
cars_available.use_when: Client has owned the position, CARs were normal but asymmetries present

cars_available.notes: Capacity-focused hip CAR in 90/90 - high irradiation, full body tension, slowest rep
T-spine rotation in 90/90 - pelvis is locked, rotation has to come from thorax
cars_available.use_when: T-spine rotation restriction flagged, especially in desk-bound clients

cars_available.notes: Articular focus T-spine rotation from a locked pelvis base

pails_rails_targets_available.how_it_sets_up_in_this_position: Front leg IS the setup for anterior capsule PAIL/RAIL - the leg is already in the stretched ER-in-flexion position. Client sits in 90/90, then actively pushes the front shin down into the floor (PAIL - contracting the stretched ER) or lifts the shin off the floor (RAIL - contracting the antagonist to drive deeper ER).
pails_rails_targets_available.level_programming: L1 for new clients at 20-40% effort, 2 rounds of 10s/10s. L2 at 50-70% once owned.

pails_rails_targets_available.how_it_sets_up_in_this_position: BACK leg IS the setup for posterior capsule PAIL/RAIL - already in IR-in-flexion end range. Client pushes back shin down into floor (PAIL - contracting stretched IR) or lifts the shin to drive deeper IR (RAIL).
pails_rails_targets_available.level_programming: L1 for new clients. Progress to L2-L3 once owned.

pails_rails_targets_available.how_it_sets_up_in_this_position: Rotate to end range T-spine rotation toward the front leg. Client pushes hands into floor behind them (PAIL - rotation antagonist drive) or actively rotates further (RAIL).
pails_rails_targets_available.level_programming: L1-L2 for most clients. Combining spine + hip PAIL/RAIL in same session requires L2+ mastery.

end_range_iso_positions.description: Hold the 90/90 front leg in full ER with active glute engagement. Iso hold, 20-45 seconds.
end_range_iso_positions.progression_code: PRH (Passive Range Hold) -> PRLO (Passive Range Loaded)
end_range_iso_positions.library_status: TO BE BUILT in End-Range Iso library

end_range_iso_positions.description: Hold the 90/90 back leg in full IR with pelvis upright. Static hold challenges posterior capsule capacity.
Lift the front shin 1-2 inches off floor and hold. Active end-range hip ER capacity.

hovers_available.description: Hover the front leg shin slightly off the floor while maintaining position. Tests active hip ER at end range.
hovers_available.library_status: TO BE BUILT in Hovers library

hovers_available.description: Hover the back leg thigh slightly up - tests active hip IR capacity at end range.

ramping_opportunities.description: Isometric ramping in 90/90 - graded increases in effort from 20% to 80% across 5-8 reps. Indicated if spasm finding on assessment.
ramping_opportunities.library_status: TO BE BUILT in Isometric Ramping library

live_in_the_position_work.name: Pelvic Rocking (forward/back)
live_in_the_position_work.description: In 90/90, gently rock the pelvis forward and back - exploring the anterior/posterior tilt range while the legs stay fixed.
live_in_the_position_work.purpose: Pelvic awareness, hip flexion capacity, spine integration

live_in_the_position_work.name: Side-to-Side Rocking
live_in_the_position_work.description: Shift weight from sit bone to sit bone in 90/90. Identifies asymmetries and trains glute activation at end range.
live_in_the_position_work.purpose: Pelvic control, asymmetry awareness, side-to-side ROM

live_in_the_position_work.name: T-Spine Reach-Arounds
live_in_the_position_work.description: In 90/90, reach one arm behind and around to open the ribcage. Thoracic rotation with hips locked.
live_in_the_position_work.purpose: T-spine rotation from a locked-hip base

live_in_the_position_work.name: Forward Fold over Front Leg
live_in_the_position_work.description: Slowly fold forward over the front leg from 90/90. Loads hip ER in flexion under gravity.
live_in_the_position_work.purpose: Passive capsule loading, breath work at end range

live_in_the_position_work.name: Hand-Walk Transitions
live_in_the_position_work.description: Transition from 90/90 to other seated positions (long sit, butterfly) using hands to support the change. Trains fluidity between positions.
live_in_the_position_work.purpose: Dynamic control, capacity to flow between shapes

live_in_the_position_work.name: Breath Work at End Range
live_in_the_position_work.description: In any end-range position in 90/90, perform 5-10 slow breaths. Trains nervous system to relax at restricted ranges.
live_in_the_position_work.purpose: Parasympathetic drive, restriction release, psychological tolerance

live_in_the_position_work.name: Talking Through (Client Coaching Phase)
live_in_the_position_work.description: Coach talks the client through what they're feeling in the position while they live in it. 'Where do you feel it?' 'What shifted?' 'Breathe into that spot.'
live_in_the_position_work.purpose: Client awareness, mind-body connection, coach-client feedback loop

typical_block_dose.beginner_client: 5-8 minutes - introduce the position, one or two CARs, breath work, short forward fold
typical_block_dose.intermediate_client: 10-15 minutes - full CAR work, PAILs/RAILs on one capsular target, live-in work, T-spine integration
typical_block_dose.advanced_client: 15-20 minutes - multi-target PAILs/RAILs, end-range isos, hovers, rocking work, deep live-in
typical_block_dose.kinstretch_flow_class: 8-12 minutes - one position in a flow of 4-6 positions

flow_partners.why_pairs: Pigeon deepens hip flexion + ER after 90/90 - natural progression within a flow
Butterfly opens the adductors and hip flexion differently than 90/90 - complementary seated position
Bear sit (feet wide, hands between legs) offers a recovery position and different hip demand between 90/90 sides
Long sit extends the hip into flexion with knees straight - nervous system contrast after bent-knee work
Cossack takes the hip into loaded ER/IR at the same time - natural strength progression from 90/90 capacity work

prescribe_this_position_when: Hip IR or ER flagged as restricted (aberrant CAR or table test)
T-spine rotation restriction in desk-bound clients (locked pelvis forces T-spine to work)
Sessions focused on hip capacity building
Client has plateaued in standing hip work and needs a loaded seated capsule focus
avoid_this_position_when: Acute knee or hip symptoms (see contraindications)
Client unable to get to the floor safely
pair_with_external_strength_work: Squat pattern blocks - 90/90 opens the hips for loaded squatting
Hinge blocks - hip capacity work carries to loaded RDLs
Any lower body session with hip-flagged findings

number_4.name: Number 4 (Figure 4)
number_4.description: Ankle of one leg crossed over the opposite knee to form a number '4' shape. Can be done seated (spine upright, opposite foot on floor), supine (lying on back, pulling opposite leg toward chest), or standing (balanced on one leg, other leg crossed in figure-4). Unilateral hip ER focus - the crossed leg's hip is loaded in external rotation.
setup_cues: Ankle of working leg crosses over opposite thigh (just above the knee)
Working leg's shin approximately parallel to the floor or thigh
Supporting leg: seated = foot flat on floor, supine = pulled toward chest, standing = grounded and stable
Working hip's knee drifts out to the side - that's hip ER. Encourage it, don't force it.
Ankle stays flexed (dorsiflexed) to protect the knee
primary_capacities_trained: Pelvic control during unilateral hip loading
number_4.entry_criteria: Can sit or lie down without acute back pain. Can cross leg over without knee pain. Ankle flexion cleared.
contraindications: Acute knee pain on the crossed leg side
Piriformis syndrome acute phase (paradoxically - figure 4 often prescribed for this, but acute flare should be avoided)

regressions.name: Supine Figure 4 (gravity-assisted)
regressions.when_to_use: Seated figure 4 loads too much. Lying on back removes gravity loading on spine, allows gentler hip ER.

regressions.name: Modified Figure 4 with Strap
regressions.when_to_use: Client can't reach the supporting leg to pull it toward chest. Use yoga strap around supporting thigh.

regressions.name: Seated Figure 4 on Chair
regressions.when_to_use: Client can't get to floor. Chair-seated version - cross ankle over opposite knee while sitting in chair.

progressions.name: Seated Figure 4 Forward Fold
progressions.what_it_adds: Loads hip ER under gravity + spine flexion demand
progressions.gates_to_move_up: Owns upright seated figure 4 for 60s per side

progressions.name: Figure 4 with Supporting Leg Lift
progressions.what_it_adds: Active hip flexion on supporting leg while maintaining figure 4 - harder pelvic control

progressions.name: Standing Figure 4 (pistol squat prep)
progressions.what_it_adds: Balance + unilateral loading + hip ER at depth - advanced capacity demand
progressions.gates_to_move_up: Owns supine + seated figure 4. Pistol squat progression active.

cars_available.notes: Modified hip CARs adapted to figure 4 - working hip explores ER through its range
cars_available.use_when: Introduction to isolated hip ER work

cars_available.notes: Similar articular demand to 90/90 front leg - explore ER-in-flexion capsular edges

pails_rails_targets_available.how_it_sets_up_in_this_position: Figure 4 loads the anterior capsule unilaterally. PAIL = press the crossed-leg knee DOWN toward the floor (contract stretched ER). RAIL = drive the knee AWAY from midline (ER contraction to deepen the position).
pails_rails_targets_available.level_programming: L1-L3 based on tolerance. Figure 4 often easier than 90/90 for introducing the PAIL/RAIL concept.

end_range_iso_positions.description: Active hold of figure 4 with maximum hip ER engagement - 20-45s

hovers_available.description: Hover the crossed-leg knee slightly off the supporting thigh - active ER hold at end range

live_in_the_position_work.name: Slow Forward Fold
live_in_the_position_work.description: From seated figure 4, fold forward. Lets hip ER load under gravity.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Supporting Leg Pull-Toward-Chest (Supine)
live_in_the_position_work.description: From supine figure 4, pull the supporting leg gently toward chest. Increases the hip ER on the crossed leg.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Crossed-Leg Knee Press
live_in_the_position_work.description: Press the crossed-leg knee away from midline with the hand - passive ER deepening.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Talking Through
live_in_the_position_work.description: Where does the client feel it? Glute, outer hip, anterior hip? Tells you where the restriction lives.
live_in_the_position_work.purpose: Diagnostic info for next session programming

flow_partners.why_pairs: Figure 4 and pigeon both load anterior hip capsule - figure 4 is lighter intro, pigeon deeper
Supine figure 4 IS a regression of this position - pair for deep recovery
Both load hip ER; figure 4 is unilateral/isolated, 90/90 is bilateral/symmetric
Progression path - seated figure 4 to standing figure 4 (pistol prep)

prescribe_this_position_when: Hip ER restriction isolated to one side (asymmetric)
Introduction to hip ER work for new clients
avoid_this_position_when: Acute knee pain crossed leg side
pair_with_external_strength_work: Unilateral lower body days (split squat, RFESS)
Cool-down after heavy squat or lunge work
Any session with unilateral hip asymmetry flagged

pigeon.name: Pigeon
pigeon.description: Front leg bent at the knee, shin angled across the body in hip external rotation and flexion. Back leg extended straight behind, hip in extension, toes pointed. Torso can be upright or folded forward. Classic hip opener that loads anterior capsule of the back hip and external rotators of the front hip simultaneously.
setup_cues: Front shin angled across the mat - more parallel to the front = more ER demand
Back leg extended straight behind, hip pointing down to floor
Hips square toward the front of the mat - don't let the back hip open
Torso upright to start; fold forward for deeper capsular loading
Support hands on the floor or a block - don't grind into the knee
primary_capacities_trained: Front hip external rotation + flexion (anterior capsule)
Back hip extension (anterior hip / hip flexor capsule)
Torso control with asymmetric hip position
pigeon.entry_criteria: Can sit in 90/90 for 60 seconds on both sides without knee pain. Can tolerate knee flexion beyond 90 degrees passively. If knee pain prevents the position, regress immediately.
contraindications: Acute knee pain - especially medial knee (pigeon loads the inside of the front knee)
Meniscus flare - pigeon compresses the knee joint
Acute hip labral flare (anterior impingement common in this position)

regressions.name: Supine Figure-4 (Threading the Needle)
regressions.when_to_use: Client can't tolerate weight on the front knee. Lie on back, cross ankle over opposite knee, pull the supporting leg toward chest.

regressions.name: Pigeon on Box or Bench
regressions.when_to_use: Client can't get to floor or has hip restriction preventing full depth. Front shin on a box, back leg extended behind on floor.

regressions.name: Half-Pigeon with Folded Blanket
regressions.when_to_use: Hip ER restriction causes back hip to lift off floor. Fold blanket/towel under the front hip for support.

progressions.name: Pigeon Forward Fold
progressions.what_it_adds: Loads anterior capsule of front hip under gravity. Increases depth of ER-in-flexion stretch.
progressions.gates_to_move_up: Owns upright pigeon for 60s without knee or back pain

progressions.name: Pigeon with Back Leg Quad Stretch
progressions.what_it_adds: Adds hip extension + knee flexion on the back leg - loads back hip anterior capsule and quad
progressions.gates_to_move_up: Owns pigeon forward fold. No knee restriction on back leg.

progressions.name: Active Pigeon Lift-Offs (front hip off floor)
progressions.what_it_adds: Active hip ER strength at end range - lifts the front shin off floor in full pigeon position
progressions.gates_to_move_up: Owns pigeon forward fold + back leg quad stretch. Active control at end range.

cars_available.notes: Modified hip CAR in pigeon position - front leg explores ER in flexion throughout
Articular focus - explore the restricted ranges of front hip ER

pails_rails_targets_available.target_name: Anterior Capsule (front hip - ER in flexion)
pails_rails_targets_available.how_it_sets_up_in_this_position: Pigeon is THE anterior capsule setup. Front leg is already in full ER-in-flexion. PAIL = push front shin down into floor (contract the stretched ER). RAIL = lift shin off floor to drive deeper ER.
pails_rails_targets_available.level_programming: L1-L3 based on tolerance. Pigeon loads the capsule more intensely than 90/90 - start conservative.

pails_rails_targets_available.how_it_sets_up_in_this_position: Back leg is in full hip extension. PAIL = push the back thigh down into floor (contract hip flexors against the stretched position). RAIL = lift the back thigh off floor to drive deeper extension.
pails_rails_targets_available.level_programming: L1-L2 - most clients have hip flexor restriction, start conservative.

end_range_iso_positions.description: Hold pigeon with active glute engagement on front hip - iso hold 30-60s
end_range_iso_positions.progression_code: PRH -> PRLO (add load overhead or to front thigh)

end_range_iso_positions.description: Active hip extension hold on back leg while in pigeon

hovers_available.description: Hover front leg slightly off floor - active hip ER at end range

ramping_opportunities.description: Iso ramping in pigeon - graded effort from 20-80% over 5-8 reps

live_in_the_position_work.name: Forward Fold Over Front Leg
live_in_the_position_work.description: Slowly lower torso forward over the front leg. Passive loading of the front hip capsule.
live_in_the_position_work.purpose: Passive capsule loading, breath work at end range

live_in_the_position_work.name: Pigeon Rocking
live_in_the_position_work.description: Shift weight forward and back subtly in the pigeon position - finds the edges of front hip restriction.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Back Leg Quad Pull-In
live_in_the_position_work.description: Reach back, grab back ankle, pull heel toward glute. Adds back hip extension + knee flexion simultaneously.
live_in_the_position_work.purpose: Back hip anterior capsule + quad lengthening

live_in_the_position_work.name: Side-to-Side Weight Shift
live_in_the_position_work.description: Shift weight onto the side of the front hip, then back. Explores different aspects of hip ER.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Breath Work at Deep Fold
live_in_the_position_work.description: In deep pigeon fold, 5-10 slow breaths. Let the tissue release under gravity.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Talking Through
live_in_the_position_work.description: Coach dialogue while client is in the shape - 'where do you feel it, what shifted, how's breathing going.'

typical_block_dose.beginner_client: 5-8 min per side - introduce position, forward fold, breath work
typical_block_dose.intermediate_client: 10-15 min per side - full CARs + PAILs/RAILs + fold work
typical_block_dose.advanced_client: 15-20 min per side - multi-modal with quad stretch, ramping, deep live-in

flow_partners.why_pairs: 90/90 warms the hips bilaterally; pigeon takes the same direction deeper unilaterally
Runner's lunge and pigeon both load the back hip anterior capsule - complementary
Butterfly balances the ER-focused pigeon with bilateral adductor work
Supine recovery after intense pigeon loading - figure-4 stretch as integration
Couch stretch isolates the back hip capsule that pigeon loads - same target, different mechanical angle

prescribe_this_position_when: Hip external rotation OR hip extension flagged as restricted
Anterior hip capsule restriction on assessment (CAJP positive)
Chronic hip flexor tightness in desk-bound clients
Block intent includes deep hip capacity work
avoid_this_position_when: Acute knee pain (pigeon loads the knee medially)
pair_with_external_strength_work: Squat pattern blocks - pigeon opens the hips for deeper squatting
Split squat / RFESS blocks - pigeon trains the capsular demands of unilateral lower body
Any session where hip flexor tightness is limiting performance

bear_sit.name: Bear Sit
bear_sit.description: Seated on the floor with feet wide, knees bent and pulled up, heels on the floor, torso forward. The forearms or hands rest between the legs or on the floor in front. Loads deep hip flexion + abduction + ankle dorsiflexion simultaneously. Position of recovery between harder positions in a flow, also a capacity position itself.
setup_cues: Feet wider than shoulders, heels flat
Knees bent and pulled up, tracking out over toes
Torso leans forward between the thighs
Arms between legs or on floor in front
Spine rounded is fine in bear sit (unlike deep squat, the spine fold is expected)
primary_capacities_trained: Recovery position - passive loading of multiple joints
bear_sit.entry_criteria: Can deep squat with heels on the floor or close to it. Ankle DF sufficient to sit with heels down.
contraindications: Acute low back pain (if flexion intolerant)

regressions.name: Bear Sit with Heels Elevated
regressions.when_to_use: Ankle DF prevents heels on floor. Use small plates or folded mat under heels.

regressions.name: Supported Bear Sit (hands or forearms on blocks)
regressions.when_to_use: Forward fold too intense, back rounds hard. Elevate hand support.

regressions.name: Narrow Bear Sit
regressions.when_to_use: Hip ER restriction prevents wide stance. Start narrower, progress wider.

progressions.name: Bear Sit Rocks (forward/back)
progressions.what_it_adds: Dynamic loading of hip flexion + ankle DF

progressions.name: Bear Sit with Arm Reaches
progressions.what_it_adds: Adds shoulder flexion and T-spine extension while in deep hip flexion

progressions.name: Bear Sit to Deep Squat Stand
progressions.what_it_adds: Transition from bear sit up to deep squat (still with hips in flexion but standing on feet) - integrates strength
progressions.gates_to_move_up: Owns bear sit + rocks. Deep squat pattern acceptable.

cars_available.notes: Modified for bear sit - hips already in deep flexion + abduction

pails_rails_targets_available.how_it_sets_up_in_this_position: Bear sit loads deep hip flexion passively. PAIL = press feet into floor and drive hips into deeper flexion. RAIL = lift thighs off floor (hip flexors contract to deepen).
pails_rails_targets_available.level_programming: L1-L2

end_range_iso_positions.description: Active bear sit - press feet into floor, pull torso forward, active engagement

live_in_the_position_work.name: Bear Sit Rocks (forward/back)
live_in_the_position_work.description: Rock torso forward (deeper fold) and back (less fold). Loads variably.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Side-to-Side Weight Shift
live_in_the_position_work.description: Shift weight onto one heel, then the other. Unilateral hip demand from bilateral position.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Arm Reach Forward
live_in_the_position_work.description: Reach arms forward on the floor while in bear sit. T-spine extension + shoulder flexion.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Breath at Deep Fold
live_in_the_position_work.description: In deepest bear sit fold, deep breathing. Passive recovery.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Stand to Deep Squat
live_in_the_position_work.description: From bear sit, press feet into floor, stand up into a deep squat. Integrated strength work.

typical_block_dose.beginner_client: 3-5 min - primarily recovery between other positions
typical_block_dose.intermediate_client: 5-8 min - can include CARs and rock work
typical_block_dose.advanced_client: 6-10 min - fuller integration with rocks and transitions
typical_block_dose.kinstretch_flow_class: 3-5 min in flow as recovery / reset position

flow_partners.why_pairs: Bear sit is a seated version of deep squat - sequence them to build squat tolerance
Both load hip ER in flexion but at different depths - butterfly superficial, bear sit deep
Both are wide-leg positions but straddle extends legs, bear sit flexes them
Bear sit is a recovery / transition position after 90/90 intensity

prescribe_this_position_when: Recovery position between intense Kinstretch positions
avoid_this_position_when: Ankle DF severely restricted (heels can't approach floor)
pair_with_external_strength_work: Squat pattern blocks - bear sit prepares the deep squat
Recovery days after intense lower body training

tall_kneeling.name: Tall Kneeling
tall_kneeling.description: Both knees on floor, hips fully extended, torso upright and stacked over knees. Tops of the feet flat or curled under (toes dorsiflexed). Foundational anti-extension / anti-rotation position that locks out the lower body and forces the trunk to do the work. The Pavel-influenced teaching position for trunk integration.
setup_cues: Both knees down, hip-width apart
Hips fully extended - glutes on, pelvis neutral (tucked slightly)
Ribs stacked directly over the pelvis
Chest tall, shoulders relaxed, crown of head tall
Tops of feet either flat on floor or curled under (toes tucked) for ankle DF demand
primary_capacities_trained: Hip extension at end range (glute activation)
Anti-extension core demand (no low back compensation)
Shoulder and overhead work with stable base
tall_kneeling.entry_criteria: Can tolerate weight on knees for 2-3 minutes without pain. If knee pain prevents the position, use padding or regress to seated.
contraindications: Toe or forefoot acute injury (if toes tucked)

regressions.name: Tall Kneeling on Pad
regressions.when_to_use: Knee discomfort on bare floor. Use yoga mat, pad, or folded towel under knees.
regressions.rebuilds_to: Unpadded tall kneeling if pain resolves

regressions.name: Seated Tall (chair or bench)
regressions.when_to_use: Knees cannot tolerate any kneeling. Use seated position - most of the same trunk demands apply.

regressions.name: Tall Kneeling with Foam Roller Under Tops of Feet
regressions.when_to_use: Client can't tuck toes and feet flat is uncomfortable. Roller under tops of feet compromises.

progressions.name: Tall Kneeling with Overhead Reach
progressions.what_it_adds: Shoulder flexion demand + anti-extension challenge (preventing lumbar extension during overhead reach)
progressions.gates_to_move_up: Owns stable tall kneeling. Shoulder flexion cleared.

progressions.name: Tall Kneeling Pallof Press
progressions.what_it_adds: Anti-rotation demand in the most locked-down position possible - pure trunk work

progressions.name: Tall Kneeling Overhead Press (DB or landmine)
progressions.what_it_adds: Integrated overhead pressing with anti-extension - direct transfer to standing OHP
progressions.gates_to_move_up: Owns Pallof + overhead reach. Pressing pattern ready.

progressions.name: Tall Kneeling Chops (loaded)
progressions.what_it_adds: Rotational core demand from the locked pelvis - teaches trunk to rotate, hips to stay still

cars_available.notes: Adapted T-spine rotation from tall kneeling - pelvis locked forces pure T-spine rotation
cars_available.use_when: T-spine rotation work with maximum pelvis lock

cars_available.notes: Shoulder CARs work well from tall kneeling - removes lower-body compensation
Articular focus from locked-pelvis base

pails_rails_targets_available.how_it_sets_up_in_this_position: Tall kneeling itself IS hip extension. PAIL = tuck pelvis harder, glutes drive contraction (hip extensors work against stretched position). RAIL = actively drive pelvis forward to deepen hip extension.
pails_rails_targets_available.level_programming: L1-L2 for most clients - usually foundational teaching of hip extension capacity

pails_rails_targets_available.how_it_sets_up_in_this_position: Pelvis locked. Rotate to end-range T-spine rotation. PAIL = rotation antagonist drive. RAIL = active rotation deeper.
pails_rails_targets_available.level_programming: L1-L3 based on mastery

end_range_iso_positions.description: Active glute contraction in tall kneeling - iso hip extension hold

live_in_the_position_work.name: Pelvic Tuck-Untuck
live_in_the_position_work.description: In tall kneeling, alternate between anterior and posterior pelvic tilt. Explore hip extension range.

live_in_the_position_work.name: T-Spine Reach-Arounds
live_in_the_position_work.description: Keeping pelvis locked, reach one arm around and up. Thoracic rotation from locked base.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Active Hip Extension Hold
live_in_the_position_work.description: Squeeze glutes, tuck pelvis, hold the end of hip extension. Iso capacity work.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Slow Overhead Reaches
live_in_the_position_work.description: Reach arms overhead slowly, maintain ribs stacked over pelvis. Tests anti-extension.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Breath Work (tall)
live_in_the_position_work.description: In tall kneeling, deep diaphragmatic breathing. Tests breath-brace coordination.

typical_block_dose.beginner_client: 5-8 min - introduce position, basic reach-arounds, breath
typical_block_dose.advanced_client: 12-18 min - full integration: chops, lifts, overhead loading

flow_partners.why_pairs: Tall kneeling tests bilateral hip extension; half-kneeling tests unilateral
Tall kneeling is the 'locked' version; standing is the integrated version
Quadruped unloads the spine; tall kneeling loads it. Sequence as progression.
Tall kneeling tests hip extension; couch stretch loads its deeper restriction

prescribe_this_position_when: T-spine rotation isolation from hip compensation
Teaching position for bracing under load
pair_with_external_strength_work: Overhead pressing blocks - tall kneeling press is a direct progression
Any day with core integration work

half_kneeling.name: Half-Kneeling
half_kneeling.description: One knee down, opposite foot flat on floor in front, forming a 90/90 angle at both hip and knee. The back knee is in full hip extension (loaded stretch for hip flexors), the front leg is in flexion. Torso upright. One of the most programmed positions in IMS - unilateral hip extension + bilateral stability demand + direct transfer to split squat and lunge patterns.
setup_cues: Back knee on mat, hip directly under (or slightly behind) shoulder
Front foot flat, knee stacked over ankle (shin vertical)
Pelvis square to the front (not rotated)
Back glute on - drive the hip into extension
Ribs stacked over pelvis, chest tall
primary_capacities_trained: Back hip extension (anterior capsule / hip flexor lengthening)
half_kneeling.entry_criteria: Can tolerate weight on one knee for 2-3 minutes. Basic hip flexor tolerance on back leg. No acute knee pain.

regressions.name: Half-Kneeling on Pad
regressions.when_to_use: Back knee discomfort on floor

regressions.name: Shortened Stance Half-Kneeling
regressions.when_to_use: Hip flexor restriction too intense at standard stance
regressions.rebuilds_to: Standard stance as hip flexor opens

regressions.name: Half-Kneeling with Wall Support
regressions.when_to_use: Client can't balance or stabilize the torso. Kneel facing a wall, use it for support.

progressions.name: Half-Kneeling Press / Pull
progressions.what_it_adds: Loaded upper body work in a position that challenges both hip extension (back) and anti-rotation
progressions.gates_to_move_up: Owns static half-kneeling for 60s per side

progressions.name: Half-Kneeling Chops / Lifts
progressions.what_it_adds: Rotational core demand + unilateral hip stability - one of the highest-value core exercises

progressions.name: Half-Kneeling Hip Flexor Mobilization (active)
progressions.what_it_adds: Graded hip flexor loading with active glute drive

cars_available.notes: Modified for half-kneeling - back leg explores hip extension range
T-spine rotation from half-kneeling - half-kneeling locks the hips, T-spine does the work
Articular focus T-spine rotation from half-kneeling - standard of care for desk-bound clients

pails_rails_targets_available.how_it_sets_up_in_this_position: Back leg is in hip extension. PAIL = press back knee down into floor (hip flexors contract against stretched position). RAIL = tuck pelvis and drive back hip forward (glute drives deeper extension).
pails_rails_targets_available.level_programming: L1-L3 based on mastery - foundational hip flexor mobility + strength work

end_range_iso_positions.description: Active hip extension hold with glute engagement - iso work for back hip

live_in_the_position_work.name: Pelvic Tucks (isolated hip extension)
live_in_the_position_work.description: Tuck pelvis, hold, release. Glute activation specific to this position.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Forward Lean (deepen hip flexor stretch)
live_in_the_position_work.description: Slowly shift forward over front leg. Back hip extension increases.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Active Glute Squeeze + Hold
live_in_the_position_work.description: Maximum glute engagement on back leg, hold 10-20s.

live_in_the_position_work.name: T-Spine Rotation Reach
live_in_the_position_work.description: Reach the arm opposite the front leg across the body. T-spine rotates with stable hips.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Breath at Edge of Hip Flexor Stretch
live_in_the_position_work.description: In deep half-kneeling position, deep breaths. Let tissue release.

typical_block_dose.beginner_client: 5-8 min per side - introduce, basic holds, breath
typical_block_dose.intermediate_client: 8-12 min per side - CARs + PAILs/RAILs + integrated work
typical_block_dose.advanced_client: 10-15 min per side - full integration with loaded chops/presses

flow_partners.why_pairs: Tall kneeling bilateral; half-kneeling unilateral - natural progression
Both load back hip extension - couch deeper, half-kneeling active
Runner's lunge takes half-kneeling deeper into hip extension + flexion
Half-kneeling and pigeon both load unilateral hip work - different angles

prescribe_this_position_when: Hip extension restriction / hip flexor tightness flagged
Split squat or lunge progression preparation

couch_stretch.name: Couch Stretch
couch_stretch.description: Back knee on floor with back foot elevated on a couch, wall, or bench (foot resting on vertical surface). Front leg in a lunge position with foot flat. Torso upright. The back hip is driven into deep extension while the back knee is in deep flexion - the most intense hip flexor and quad loading position commonly programmed. Named for its use with a couch at home.
setup_cues: Back knee on floor (padded), back foot pointing straight up against vertical surface
Back shin vertical, pressed into the wall/couch
Front leg forward in lunge position, foot flat, shin vertical
Torso tall - resist the instinct to lean forward (that lets the hip flexor off the hook)
Glute ON on the back leg - drives hip into extension
primary_capacities_trained: Front hip flexion + knee flexion (lunge demand)
couch_stretch.entry_criteria: Owns half-kneeling for 60s per side. Can tolerate deep knee flexion on the back leg. Clinical hip flexor + quad tolerance.
contraindications: Acute knee pain (especially back knee - deep flexion)

regressions.name: Standard Half-Kneeling
regressions.when_to_use: Couch stretch too intense. Half-kneeling is the prerequisite - master first.

regressions.name: Couch Stretch with Back Knee NOT Against Wall
regressions.when_to_use: Full wall version too intense. Back knee on floor, back foot on a low box (6-12 inches), gentler knee flexion.

regressions.name: Bolster Under Back Ankle
regressions.when_to_use: Back ankle discomfort pressing into wall. Bolster or pillow softens the contact.

progressions.name: Couch Stretch with Torso Upright + Active Glute
progressions.what_it_adds: True position - most adults lean forward to escape. Tall torso + active glute is the full demand.
progressions.gates_to_move_up: Can hold the tall torso for 60s per side without leaning

progressions.name: Couch Stretch with Arm Reach Overhead
progressions.what_it_adds: Shoulder flexion + anti-extension + deep hip extension simultaneously

progressions.name: Couch Stretch with Pelvic Tuck
progressions.what_it_adds: Active hip extension engagement - glute drives pelvic tuck, deepens the stretch

pails_rails_targets_available.how_it_sets_up_in_this_position: Couch stretch loads the anterior capsule more intensely than any other base position. PAIL = drive back knee forward into floor (hip flexors contract against stretched position). RAIL = squeeze glute + drive pelvis forward (glute drives deeper hip extension).
pails_rails_targets_available.level_programming: L1-L3 based on mastery. This is the capsular target for hip flexor tightness.

end_range_iso_positions.description: Tall torso + active glute squeeze in couch stretch - 30-60s hold

ramping_opportunities.description: Graded glute contraction 20-80% in couch stretch - indicated for hip flexor spasm finding

live_in_the_position_work.name: Active Glute Squeeze + Hold
live_in_the_position_work.description: In couch stretch, maximum glute engagement on back leg.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Pelvic Tuck (drive pelvis forward)
live_in_the_position_work.description: Tuck pelvis, push hips forward - deepens hip extension.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Slow Tall-Up
live_in_the_position_work.description: Start torso leaned forward (easier), slowly rise to fully upright. Graded tolerance build.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Breath at the Edge
live_in_the_position_work.description: In full couch stretch (tall torso), breathe deeply. Let tissue release.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Arm Reach Overhead
live_in_the_position_work.description: Reach arms overhead in couch stretch. Integrate shoulder flexion with hip extension.

typical_block_dose.beginner_client: 3-5 min per side - introduce position, short holds
typical_block_dose.intermediate_client: 5-8 min per side - full PAIL/RAIL work
typical_block_dose.advanced_client: 8-12 min per side - multi-modal with integration

flow_partners.why_pairs: Half-kneeling is the prerequisite; couch deepens it
Pigeon loads back leg extension at knee straight; couch at knee bent - different angles same target
Runner's lunge is dynamic; couch is the held, most-intense version
Tall kneeling active bilateral hip extension; couch unilateral deep hip extension

prescribe_this_position_when: Deep hip flexor restriction - the primary target
Desk-bound clients with anterior pelvic tilt
pair_with_external_strength_work: Squat pattern days (couch stretch opens hip flexors for squat depth)

runners_lunge.name: Runner's Lunge
runners_lunge.description: Deep lunge position with back knee on floor (or hovered), front foot flat, front knee over ankle or slightly forward. Torso can be upright with hands on front thigh or hanging forward with hands on floor beside front foot. A dynamic-capable version of the half-kneeling / couch stretch family. Often used as a transition between standing and floor positions, and as an active hip flexor + hamstring opener.
setup_cues: Front foot flat, knee stacked over ankle or slightly forward
Back knee on floor (rest) or hovered (active)
Front shin angle varies - vertical for balanced load, forward for deeper ankle DF demand
Torso can be upright or folded forward over front leg
Back leg actively engaged, not passive
primary_capacities_trained: Dynamic position - transitions well in flows
runners_lunge.entry_criteria: Can lunge with bodyweight cleanly. No acute knee or hip pain. Basic tolerance for deep lunge position.

regressions.name: Runner's Lunge on Pad
regressions.when_to_use: Back knee discomfort on floor

regressions.name: Shortened Stance Runner's Lunge
regressions.when_to_use: Hip flexor restriction prevents long stance

regressions.name: Runner's Lunge with Blocks Under Hands
regressions.when_to_use: Client can't reach floor with hands in forward-fold version. Blocks elevate.

progressions.name: Runner's Lunge with Thoracic Rotation
progressions.what_it_adds: T-spine rotation + open the ribcage while in hip flexion/extension asymmetry

progressions.name: Runner's Lunge to Standing (Flowing)
progressions.what_it_adds: Dynamic strength transition - comes out of the lunge fluidly

progressions.name: Runner's Lunge with Back Knee Hovered
progressions.what_it_adds: Active hip extension on back leg (quad + glute engaged) - isometric loading

cars_available.notes: Hip CARs adapted for runner's lunge - similar to half-kneeling but with more forward weight
cars_available.use_when: Hip work from a lunge position

pails_rails_targets_available.how_it_sets_up_in_this_position: Same as half-kneeling and couch stretch - back hip in extension. Slightly different angle due to forward torso lean option.
pails_rails_targets_available.level_programming: L1-L2

pails_rails_targets_available.how_it_sets_up_in_this_position: Front hip is in deep flexion with extended-leg option (straighten front leg). PAIL = press front leg into floor. RAIL = lift front leg actively.
pails_rails_targets_available.level_programming: L1-L2

end_range_iso_positions.description: Back knee hovered hold - active quad + glute - 15-30s

hovers_available.description: Hover back knee 1-2 inches off floor while in runner's lunge

live_in_the_position_work.name: Forward / Backward Rocking
live_in_the_position_work.description: Rock pelvis forward (deeper back hip extension) and back (shifts load to front hip).

live_in_the_position_work.name: Thoracic Rotation Reach
live_in_the_position_work.description: Reach the arm same-side as the front leg up and over. T-spine opens.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Back Knee Hover
live_in_the_position_work.description: Lift back knee 1-2 inches off floor. Active engagement of back leg.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Front Leg Extension (reach)
live_in_the_position_work.description: Straighten the front leg to turn runner's lunge into a hamstring stretch.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Breath at Edge
live_in_the_position_work.description: In deepest runner's lunge, breath work at end range.

typical_block_dose.kinstretch_flow_class: 3-5 min per side - often transition position

flow_partners.why_pairs: Half-kneeling is static, runner's lunge is dynamic - complementary
Runner's lunge dynamic version of couch - different angles of hip extension
Both unilateral hip work - sequence from dynamic to deep
Runner's lunge transitions fluidly to standing - good flow partner

prescribe_this_position_when: Transition position between standing and floor work

standing.name: Standing
standing.description: Upright standing position with feet hip-width, weight distributed evenly, spine stacked, shoulders neutral. The integrated position - where all FRC capacity earned in floor positions must express itself under gravity. Most CARs and mobility work eventually gets tested in standing. Also the default position for external strength work.
setup_cues: Feet hip-width apart, weight in the middle of each foot
Knees soft, not locked
Pelvis neutral - ribs stacked over pelvis
Shoulders relaxed, arms at sides
Crown of head tall, chin level
primary_capacities_trained: Shoulder CARs and mobility (expressed under gravity)
Spine CARs with integrated hip stability
standing.entry_criteria: Any client who can stand for 5+ minutes without pain or balance failure.
contraindications: Acute vertigo / vestibular issues (avoid unsupported standing CARs)

regressions.name: Standing with Wall Support
regressions.when_to_use: Balance issues or client recovering from vestibular event. Back against wall.

regressions.name: Standing with Hand on Post/Rack
regressions.when_to_use: Need light balance support for CARs or PAILs/RAILs work

regressions.name: Standing with Wider Stance
