progressions.gates_to_move_up: Can stand on one leg eyes open for 30+ seconds

progressions.name: Single-Leg Standing Eyes Closed
progressions.what_it_adds: Proprioception + vestibular challenge

progressions.name: Standing on Unstable Surface (foam pad)
progressions.what_it_adds: Reactive stabilization demand

cars_available.notes: Default shoulder CAR position - integrates with gravity
Articular focus shoulder work from standing
Capacity shoulder work with full-body irradiation
Standing hip CAR - baseline articular screen
Neck CAR standing - postural demand on spine while moving head
Scap CARs from standing - isolated scap motion

live_in_the_position_work.name: Standing Breath Work
live_in_the_position_work.description: Quiet standing with breath focus. Posture check.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Single-Leg Balance Progressions
live_in_the_position_work.description: Progress through eyes open -> eyes closed -> unstable surface on one leg.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Standing Walk Check
live_in_the_position_work.description: Walk slowly across the studio, then back. Coach observes for gait asymmetries.
live_in_the_position_work.purpose: Gait diagnostic + integration of other position work

live_in_the_position_work.name: Wall Slides
live_in_the_position_work.description: Back against wall, arms slide up in goalpost - tests overhead capacity integrated.

typical_block_dose.intermediate_client: 8-12 min - multiple CAR variations, balance work
typical_block_dose.advanced_client: 10-15 min - full body CAR sequence + balance progressions
typical_block_dose.kinstretch_flow_class: 4-6 min in flow - often opening or closing position

flow_partners.why_pairs: Standing vs half-kneeling - bilateral vs unilateral hip demand
Standing transitions to deep squat - Kinstretch sequence common
Standing flows into runner's lunge, back to standing

prescribe_this_position_when: Any session - standing is the default position

squat.name: Deep Squat (Passive Hold)
squat.description: Deep squat with heels on the floor, hips below knees, torso upright. Held as a passive position rather than an active squat exercise. Tests hip flexion, ankle dorsiflexion, and T-spine extension simultaneously. Most adults have lost this position by age 30 and need weeks or months to rebuild it. Foundational squat capacity.
setup_cues: Feet slightly wider than hip-width, toes angled out 10-20 degrees
Descend slowly, heels stay flat
Hips below knees (deep)
Torso stays as upright as possible - chest up, not collapsed
Elbows can push between knees to open the hips; hands in prayer or holding a post
squat.entry_criteria: Can squat to parallel bodyweight clean. Ankle DF 25+ degrees. Hip flexion 100+ degrees. If any of these fail, use regressions.
contraindications: Acute knee pain (deep squat loads knee heavily)

regressions.name: Deep Squat Holding TRX or Post
regressions.when_to_use: Balance or depth insufficient. Hold strap to descend gradually.

regressions.name: Heel-Elevated Deep Squat
regressions.when_to_use: Ankle DF prevents heels on floor. Small plates/wedges under heels.

regressions.name: Partial Deep Squat (parallel)
regressions.when_to_use: Full depth inaccessible. Stop at parallel for now.

regressions.name: Seated Deep Squat (on low box)
regressions.when_to_use: Can't hold depth passively. Sit on small box at deep squat height.

progressions.name: Deep Squat with T-Spine Reach
progressions.what_it_adds: Thoracic rotation while in full hip flexion - integrated demand

progressions.name: Deep Squat to Stand (slow)
progressions.what_it_adds: Strength out of deep position - end-range squat strength

progressions.name: Loaded Deep Squat (goblet)
progressions.what_it_adds: Loaded loading of end-range squat capacity
progressions.gates_to_move_up: Owns unloaded deep squat + slow stand

cars_available.notes: Modified for deep squat - pelvis locked in flexion, hip explores

pails_rails_targets_available.how_it_sets_up_in_this_position: Deep squat is the loaded end-range of hip flexion with abduction. PAIL = push feet into floor, drive out of the squat (hip extensors contract against stretched position). RAIL = pull hips deeper into flexion with hands on ankles or ground.
pails_rails_targets_available.level_programming: L1-L3 based on mastery

pails_rails_targets_available.how_it_sets_up_in_this_position: Deep squat loads DF with full hip flexion. PAIL = drive big toe into floor (calf contracts in DF). RAIL = lift toes + drive knee forward (tibialis drives deeper DF).
pails_rails_targets_available.level_programming: L1-L2

end_range_iso_positions.description: Active deep squat - knees drive out, chest up, 30-60s

live_in_the_position_work.name: Deep Squat Rocking (side to side)
live_in_the_position_work.description: Shift weight from foot to foot at depth.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Knee Push-Outs (with elbows)
live_in_the_position_work.description: Use elbows to push knees wider. Opens hip ER at depth.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Arm Reach Overhead
live_in_the_position_work.description: Reach arms overhead while in deep squat. Tests T-spine extension at depth.

live_in_the_position_work.name: T-Spine Rotation
live_in_the_position_work.description: In deep squat, rotate torso to look over each shoulder.
live_in_the_position_work.purpose: Spine rotation at deep hip flexion

live_in_the_position_work.name: Breath at Depth
live_in_the_position_work.description: In deep squat, deep breathing. Parasympathetic at end range.

flow_partners.why_pairs: Bear sit is seated version; deep squat is standing version. Sequence them.
Deep squat and standing are bookends - integrate transitions
Both load hip abduction; butterfly seated, deep squat standing
Pigeon unilateral deep hip; deep squat bilateral deep hip

prescribe_this_position_when: Deep hip flexion + ankle DF restriction

supine.name: Supine
supine.description: Lying on the back with knees bent (feet flat on floor) or legs extended. Gravity-eliminated spine position - ideal for clients with acute back issues, pregnancy, or who need the lowest-load base for training. Most core work starts here. Also houses figure-4 variants and supine hip work.
setup_cues: Lying on back, arms comfortable at sides or overhead
Knees bent, feet flat (default), OR legs extended
Low back in natural curve (not pressed hard into floor)
Shoulders relaxed, head neutral
primary_capacities_trained: Supine hip work (figure-4, knee hugs, hip circles)
supine.entry_criteria: Any client who can lie down comfortably. No contraindications for supine in most cases.
contraindications: Acute severe reflux (not supine for any duration)

regressions.name: Supine with Pillow Under Knees
regressions.when_to_use: Low back discomfort with legs extended

regressions.name: Supine with Pillow Under Head
regressions.when_to_use: Cervical discomfort in flat supine

progressions.name: Supine Figure 4
progressions.what_it_adds: Hip ER work gravity-assisted

progressions.name: Dead Bug Progressions
progressions.what_it_adds: Anti-extension core work with limb movement

progressions.name: Supine Leg Raises / Lowers
progressions.what_it_adds: Hip flexion + anti-extension integration

cars_available.notes: Gravity-eliminated cervical CAR - safest option for neck work
cars_available.use_when: Cervical issues or acute neck pain clearing

cars_available.notes: Modified for supine - spine segmental work lying down

pails_rails_targets_available.how_it_sets_up_in_this_position: Supine pull-knee-to-chest is hip flexion end range. PAIL = push knee away from chest against resistance. RAIL = pull knee deeper to chest actively.
pails_rails_targets_available.level_programming: L1-L2

live_in_the_position_work.name: Supine Breath Work (diaphragmatic)
live_in_the_position_work.description: Hand on belly, deep diaphragmatic breaths. Teaches breath mechanics.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Knee Hugs to Chest
live_in_the_position_work.description: Pull one or both knees to chest. Hip flexion loading.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Supine Twist
live_in_the_position_work.description: Knees to one side, arms out in T position. Spine rotation with gravity support.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Pelvic Tilts
live_in_the_position_work.description: Flatten low back, then arch. Pelvis-spine awareness.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Supine Figure 4 Hold
live_in_the_position_work.description: Ankle over opposite knee, pull supporting leg toward chest. Hip ER work.

typical_block_dose.beginner_client: 5-10 min - breath, basic hip work, dead bug
typical_block_dose.advanced_client: 10-15 min - loaded supine work with PAIL/RAIL
typical_block_dose.kinstretch_flow_class: 4-6 min - often opening or recovery position

flow_partners.why_pairs: Supine and prone are opposite positions - flip between them in flows
Supine (unloaded) to quadruped (partial load) progression
Supine figure 4 is the regression of seated figure 4
Supine and side-lying both unloaded - complementary

prescribe_this_position_when: Acute back issues preventing upright work

prone.name: Prone
prone.description: Lying on the stomach, forehead on floor or on hands. Loads anterior body (hip flexors, quads, anterior chest) in extension. Foundational position for back extension work, prone press-ups (sphinx, cobra), and scapular work without gravity pulling the shoulder down.
setup_cues: Lying face down, forehead on floor or on stacked hands
Legs extended straight, tops of feet on floor
Pelvis neutral - pelvis drives down into floor
Arms at sides or in goalpost position depending on exercise
prone.entry_criteria: Any client who can lie prone without pain.
contraindications: Acute cervical issues (avoid prolonged forehead on floor)

regressions.name: Prone with Pillow Under Hips
regressions.when_to_use: Low back discomfort in flat prone. Pillow reduces extension.

regressions.name: Prone with Pillow Under Chest
regressions.when_to_use: Shoulder or cervical discomfort. Elevates torso slightly.

progressions.name: Prone Press-Up (Sphinx)
progressions.what_it_adds: Spine extension with arm support

progressions.name: Prone Cobra
progressions.what_it_adds: Deeper spine extension with active trunk engagement

progressions.name: Superman
progressions.what_it_adds: Full posterior chain activation - spine extension + hip extension + scapular retraction

cars_available.notes: Prone scap work - gravity doesn't pull shoulder down
Shoulder CAR with trunk glued to floor - removes compensation

pails_rails_targets_available.how_it_sets_up_in_this_position: Prone press-up loads spine extension. PAIL = press chest away from floor, hold. RAIL = lift higher into extension actively.
pails_rails_targets_available.level_programming: L1 - very conservative. Respect extension tolerance.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Prone Breath Work
live_in_the_position_work.description: In prone with forehead on hands, deep breath into the back body.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Prone Press-Ups (Sphinx)
live_in_the_position_work.description: Push up onto forearms, hips stay heavy on floor. Extension with control.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Prone Reaches (Superman variations)
live_in_the_position_work.description: Reach one arm forward, or opposite arm + leg. Posterior chain.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Prone Scap Retraction / Protraction
live_in_the_position_work.description: Lift arms slightly, retract and protract scapulae. Isolated scap work.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Prone Quadriceps Stretch
live_in_the_position_work.description: Bend one knee, grab ankle, pull heel to glute. Quad lengthening.

typical_block_dose.intermediate_client: 8-12 min - CARs, scap work, extension progressions
typical_block_dose.advanced_client: 10-15 min - superman variants, integrated posterior chain
typical_block_dose.kinstretch_flow_class: 4-6 min in flow - posterior chain complement to supine

flow_partners.why_pairs: Supine (anterior loading) and prone (posterior loading) - flip between
Prone to quadruped is a natural transition
Prone sets up the back-leg quad; couch stretch loads it standing
Supine, prone, side-lying = the three floor-lying positions

pair_with_external_strength_work: Posterior chain strength days (deadlift, bridge work)

side_lying.name: Side-Lying
side_lying.description: Lying on one side, bottom arm extended or under the head, top leg bent or extended. Gravity-assisted position that loads hip abduction, anti-lateral flexion (when active), and side-specific shoulder work. Also a regression for clients who can't tolerate supine or seated.
setup_cues: On one side, bottom arm under head or extended straight
Hips stacked vertically (don't roll forward or back)
Knees bent 90 degrees (default) or extended
Top arm resting on top hip or reaching overhead
primary_capacities_trained: Anti-lateral flexion (when held as side plank progression)
side_lying.entry_criteria: Any client who can lie on their side comfortably.
contraindications: Pregnancy (side-lying is generally recommended, but check)

regressions.name: Side-Lying with Pillow Between Knees
regressions.when_to_use: Hip discomfort or pelvic alignment issues

regressions.name: Side-Lying with Pillow Under Head
regressions.when_to_use: Neck discomfort without support

progressions.name: Side-Lying Hip Abduction (Clam)
progressions.what_it_adds: Active hip abduction / glute med activation

progressions.name: Side-Lying Leg Raises
progressions.what_it_adds: Straight-leg abduction - more demanding than clam

progressions.name: Side Plank from Side-Lying
progressions.what_it_adds: Anti-lateral flexion - huge progression

cars_available.notes: Side-lying hip CAR - gravity-assisted, great for restricted clients
cars_available.use_when: Hip ROM work when gravity upright is too demanding

cars_available.notes: Articular focus from side-lying
Shoulder CAR side-lying - gravity-eliminated for restricted shoulders
cars_available.use_when: Shoulder ROM work for restricted clients

cars_available.notes: Side-lying wrist CAR with forearm locked against torso

pails_rails_targets_available.how_it_sets_up_in_this_position: Top leg abducted, gravity wants to pull it down. PAIL = pull leg down against resistance. RAIL = drive leg higher actively.
pails_rails_targets_available.level_programming: L1-L2 for most

end_range_iso_positions.description: Top leg lifted, held in abduction - 15-30s

live_in_the_position_work.name: Clam Shells
live_in_the_position_work.description: Knees bent, top knee lifts while feet stay together. Glute med activation.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Side-Lying Leg Raises
live_in_the_position_work.description: Straight leg abduction raises.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Side-Lying Hip Rotation
live_in_the_position_work.description: In clam position, rotate knee forward and back (hip IR/ER).

live_in_the_position_work.name: Thread-the-Needle (side-lying T-spine)
live_in_the_position_work.description: Top arm reaches across body and around. T-spine rotation.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Side-Lying Shoulder CAR
live_in_the_position_work.description: Top arm performs shoulder CAR without gravity pulling it down.
live_in_the_position_work.purpose: Shoulder ROM work for restricted clients

flow_partners.why_pairs: Supine and side-lying are complementary gravity-assisted positions
Cycling through supine/side/prone covers all lying orientations
Side-lying to quadruped is a natural transition

prescribe_this_position_when: Gravity-assisted hip or shoulder CAR work for restricted clients
Clients who need floor-based work but supine is uncomfortable

quadruped.name: Quadruped
quadruped.description: On hands and knees, hands under shoulders, knees under hips, spine neutral. Partially loaded position - weight distributed through four points. Ideal for spine segmental work, thoracic rotation, hip-spine dissociation, and bird dog family. One of the most-used positions in IMS programming.
setup_cues: Hands directly under shoulders, fingers spread
Knees directly under hips
Spine neutral - not rounded or arched
Elbows soft (slight bend)
Crown of head forward, chin level
quadruped.entry_criteria: Any client without wrist or knee contraindications.
contraindications: Pregnancy later stages (position gets harder but not contraindicated)

regressions.name: Quadruped on Fists
regressions.when_to_use: Wrist pain on extension in quadruped. Fist position protects wrists.

regressions.name: Quadruped on Elbows (forearm plank variant)
regressions.when_to_use: Wrist can't tolerate any load

regressions.name: Quadruped with Pad Under Knees
regressions.when_to_use: Knee discomfort

progressions.name: Bird Dog (opposite arm + leg)
progressions.what_it_adds: Anti-rotation demand + coordination

progressions.name: Bear Position (knees hovered)
progressions.what_it_adds: Increased anti-extension + shoulder load

progressions.name: Bear Crawl (dynamic)
progressions.what_it_adds: Loaded locomotion from the position

cars_available.notes: Neck CAR from quadruped - spine loaded, neck isolated
cars_available.use_when: Cervical work with integrated spine stability

cars_available.notes: The default T-spine rotation position - isolates from lumbar
Articular focus T-spine from quadruped
Capacity T-spine work with full body tension
Spine segmental CAR - cat-cow base plus segmental exploration
Fire hydrant - unloaded hip CAR from quadruped
Scapular protraction/retraction under partial body weight
Shoulder CAR in bear position (load demanding)

pails_rails_targets_available.how_it_sets_up_in_this_position: Bird dog extended leg loads hip extension. PAIL/RAIL applied at extended position.
pails_rails_targets_available.level_programming: L1-L2

pails_rails_targets_available.how_it_sets_up_in_this_position: Quadruped rotation with bottom hand driving into floor. Pelvis locked. PAIL = rotation antagonist. RAIL = drive further rotation.
pails_rails_targets_available.level_programming: L1-L3

end_range_iso_positions.description: Bird dog opposite arm+leg extended, held 10-20s. Anti-rotation iso.
Quadruped with knees hovered, held 15-30s. Anti-extension + shoulder load.

hovers_available.description: Bird dog with longer reach and hover at end range

live_in_the_position_work.name: Cat-Cow
live_in_the_position_work.description: Spine flexion to extension, segmentally. Foundational spine mobility.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Rock and Reach
live_in_the_position_work.description: Rock hips back to heels, thread an arm under and through. T-spine + hip flexion.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Thread-the-Needle
live_in_the_position_work.description: Reach one arm under body and across. T-spine rotation from quadruped.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Bird Dog Hold
live_in_the_position_work.description: Opposite arm and leg extended, spine stable. Anti-rotation.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Slow Hip Circles
live_in_the_position_work.description: One leg lifts, traces a circle in the air. Hip mobility with spine stability.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Segmental Rocking (back and forth)
live_in_the_position_work.description: Rock forward and backward on hands and knees. Wrist and shoulder warm-up.

typical_block_dose.beginner_client: 5-8 min - basic quadruped, cat-cow, bird dog
typical_block_dose.intermediate_client: 8-12 min - full CARs + PAIL/RAIL + live-in work
typical_block_dose.advanced_client: 10-15 min - bear position progressions, loaded work
typical_block_dose.kinstretch_flow_class: 5-8 min in flow - common transition and work position

flow_partners.why_pairs: Prone to quadruped is a natural transition
Quadruped unloaded; tall kneeling loaded spine
Quadruped to bear sit - similar floor positions
Quadruped transitions to half-kneeling by bringing one leg forward

cossack.name: Cossack
cossack.description: Wide stance with weight shifted onto one bent leg (deep side squat) while the other leg extends straight out to the side with heel down and toe up. Loads the bent leg in deep hip flexion + abduction while the extended leg loads adductor length + knee extension + hip abduction. Strength and mobility simultaneously.
setup_cues: Very wide stance - wider than shoulders by a lot
Shift weight onto one leg, bend that knee deeply
Other leg stays straight, heel on floor, toe pointing up
Bent leg's knee tracks out over the toe (not caving in)
Torso upright, can hold a counterweight (KB goblet) for balance
primary_capacities_trained: Unilateral deep hip flexion + abduction (loaded leg)
cossack.entry_criteria: Can squat to parallel bodyweight clean. Can do a butterfly or straddle for 60s. Adductor tolerance sufficient.

regressions.name: Supported Cossack (holding TRX or post)
regressions.when_to_use: Balance or depth insufficient. Use support.

regressions.name: Shallower Cossack (partial depth)
regressions.when_to_use: Full depth too intense. Go to 50% depth, build over blocks.

regressions.name: Kneeling Lateral Shift
regressions.when_to_use: Can't stand in Cossack. Kneel with one leg extended to side, shift weight. Similar demand, less load.

progressions.name: Cossack Squat (active reps)
progressions.what_it_adds: Strength work - shifting side to side under tension
progressions.gates_to_move_up: Owns static Cossack for 30s per side

progressions.name: Goblet Cossack Squat
progressions.what_it_adds: Loaded version with KB or DB held at chest

progressions.name: Cossack with Overhead Load
progressions.what_it_adds: Shoulder demand + anti-lateral flexion while in Cossack

pails_rails_targets_available.how_it_sets_up_in_this_position: Extended leg's adductor is in stretched position. PAIL = press extended leg's heel down into floor (adductor contracts). RAIL = lift extended leg's heel slightly off floor (abductor drives).
pails_rails_targets_available.level_programming: L1-L2 based on tolerance

pails_rails_targets_available.how_it_sets_up_in_this_position: Loaded leg is in deep flexion + abduction. PAIL/RAIL as per deep squat protocols.
pails_rails_targets_available.level_programming: L1-L2

end_range_iso_positions.description: Static hold in deep Cossack - knee over toe, torso upright, 20-45s

live_in_the_position_work.name: Side-to-Side Cossack Shifts
live_in_the_position_work.description: Slowly shift weight from one leg to the other through the full Cossack shape.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Extended Leg Active Press
live_in_the_position_work.description: In Cossack position, actively press extended leg's heel into floor.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Depth Holds
live_in_the_position_work.description: Sit deep in Cossack on one side, hold 20-30s, breathe.

live_in_the_position_work.name: Reach-Across (T-spine rotation)
live_in_the_position_work.description: In Cossack, reach opposite hand toward the extended leg's foot. Adds spine rotation.

flow_partners.why_pairs: Butterfly prepares bilateral adductors passively; Cossack loads them unilaterally
Straddle passive wide legs; Cossack active wide legs
Cossack is frontal plane squat; sagittal squat is complement
Cossack is a loaded standing position - integrates with all standing work

prescribe_this_position_when: Athletic clients needing lateral movement prep
pair_with_external_strength_work: Squat progressions that need frontal plane variation
